name: Embolsa de Abegondo y Cecebre – Río Barcés trail
name_original: Embolsa de Abegondo y Cecebre – sendero de Río Barcés
region_intro: Hiking around Cambre, Spain, is defined by its accessible trails, primarily centered around the Cecebre Reservoir. The region features varied terrain, including paths along the reservoir's edge and routes following the Río Barcés. These hiking trails Cambre Spain offer different levels of difficulty, from easy walks to more challenging routes, suitable for diverse outdoor experiences. Best hiking trails around Cambre The most popular hiking route is Embolsa de Abegondo y Cecebre – Río Barcés trail, a 10.6 miles (17.0 km) trail that takes 4 hours 28 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows the course of the Río Barcés. Another top favourite among local hikers is Cecebre Reservoir – Cecebre Reservoir loop from Cecebre, a moderate 4.9 miles (7.9 km) path. This trail offers views of the reservoir and is generally accessible. Local hikers also love the Cecebre Reservoir – Cecebre Reservoir loop from Cambre, a 18.6 miles (29.9 km) trail leading through the reservoir's surroundings, often completed in about 7 hours 43 minutes. Hiking around Cambre is defined by reservoir paths, river trails, and varied terrain around the Cecebre Reservoir. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more difficult, longer routes. The routes in Cambre are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 340 reviews. More than 2,200 hikers have used komoot to explore Cambre's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Cambre?
A: Cambre offers a good selection of hiking trails, with over 18 routes to explore. These trails vary in difficulty, ensuring options for different fitness levels.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for hiking trails in Cambre?
A: The trails around Cambre cater to a range of abilities. You'll find 7 easy routes perfect for a leisurely stroll, 10 moderate trails for a more engaging hike, and 1 difficult route for those seeking a greater challenge. The terrain primarily features reservoir paths and river trails.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Cambre?
A: Yes, Cambre has several easy routes that are suitable for families. These trails often feature accessible paths around the Cecebre Reservoir, providing a pleasant outdoor experience for all ages.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Cambre?
A: Many trails around the Cecebre Reservoir are suitable for dogs, offering open spaces and natural environments for walks. However, it's always recommended to check specific trail regulations or local signage regarding pets before you go.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Cambre?
A: Yes, circular routes are a popular option in Cambre. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10502012" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cecebre Reservoir – Cecebre Reservoir loop from Cecebre&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 4.9-mile (7.9 km) path that offers scenic views and brings you back to your starting point. Another option is the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40016710" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cecebre Reservoir – Cecebre Reservoir loop from Cambre&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 18.6-mile (29.9 km) trail.

Q: What natural features and landmarks can I see while hiking in Cambre?
A: Hiking in Cambre centers around the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5223936" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cecebre Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the Río Barcés. You can also discover the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3901347" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riverside Beach at Cecebre Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the nearby &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1091854" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abegondo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. For historical interest, explore castles like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3532496" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de San Antón&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/422048" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Cruz Castle Island&lt;/a&gt;, which are within reach of the area.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Cambre?
A: The hiking trails in Cambre are highly regarded by the komoot community, boasting an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 340 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the varied terrain, the scenic reservoir paths, and the well-maintained river trails.

Q: Is parking available near the trailheads in Cambre?
A: Given the popularity of the Cecebre Reservoir area for outdoor activities, parking facilities are generally available near common access points and trailheads. It's advisable to check specific starting points for designated parking areas.

Q: Can I reach hiking trails in Cambre using public transport?
A: While specific public transport routes directly to all trailheads might be limited, Cambre itself is accessible by public transport. From there, you may need to walk or use local services to reach the desired starting points for hikes around the Cecebre Reservoir.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike in Cambre?
A: Hiking around Cambre, particularly the Cecebre Reservoir, is enjoyable for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and lush scenery, while summer can be warm but still suitable for early morning or late afternoon hikes. Winter can also be an option, though some paths might be wetter.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging routes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40016710" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cecebre Reservoir – Cecebre Reservoir loop from Cambre&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 18.6-mile (29.9 km) trail that takes approximately 7 hours 43 minutes to complete. This route offers significant distance and varied terrain.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trails around Cambre?
A: The terrain around Cambre is diverse, primarily featuring paths along the Cecebre Reservoir's edge and trails that follow the course of the Río Barcés. You can expect a mix of flat sections, gentle inclines, and some more rugged parts, especially on longer routes.

Q: Are there places to eat or drink near the hiking trails?
A: While the immediate trailheads might not have extensive facilities, the town of Cambre and surrounding villages offer various cafes, restaurants, and pubs where you can refuel after your hike. It's a good idea to plan your refreshment stops in advance.
tips: Just 20 minutes from A Coruña, we will find a wide sheet of water with a surprising diversity of birds: it is the Abegondo reservoir.
